# Phase 7 Ethics Compliance Plan

This planning note records Phase 7.10 institutional runtime ethics and compliance governance architecture. It is read-only and non-executing.

## Scope

- ethics governance doctrine
- compliance survivability
- regulatory interpretation continuity
- institutional risk doctrine
- runtime compliance boundaries
- governance ethics hierarchy
- compliance escalation governance
- reputational survivability
- constitutional ethics continuity
- compliance traceability
- ethics and compliance review templates
- ethics compliance blockers and escalation

## Out Of Scope

- runtime authorization
- automated compliance
- autonomous ethics systems
- machine-generated compliance enforcement
- runtime activation
- provider activation
- executable workflows
- deployment automation
- execution-capable governance systems
- regulatory automation systems

## Completion Criteria

- Ethics and compliance planning remains subordinate to human governance review.
- Ethics doctrine cannot authorize execution.
- Compliance ambiguity fails closed.
- Disputed regulatory interpretation cannot be reused until resolved by authorized human review.
- Traceability gaps block compliance interpretation use.
- Runtime-disabled truth remains preserved.
